content.text:
I can now clearly see atom O:9 in the zoomed view. Let me identify its covalently connected neighbours:

Looking at the image, O:9 (the red oxygen atom) is connected to two atoms via covalent bonds:

1. **C:10** - The carbon atom on the left side of O:9, which has 3 hydrogen atoms attached (H:11, H:12, H:13)
2. **C:41** - The carbon atom on the right side of O:9, which is part of the central ring structure

**Results:**

**Neighbour atoms of O:9:**
| Atomic Index | Element |
|--------------|---------|
| 10 | C (Carbon) |
| 41 | C (Carbon) |

**geomconf_index:** 9540

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 9, whose element should be O. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
